title: Extraction of DNA from 2 ml blood samples using Flexigene Kit (QIAGEN)
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1jfm","text":"Resuspend the lyophilized QIAGEN Protease in 1.4 ml of Buffer FG3 (hydration buffer) and store at 2–8°C or in aliquots at –20°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"10p8f","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a2ikp","text":"Frozen blood should be thawed in a 37°C water bath","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"datmg","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ef0vp","text":"For every 1 ml of blood, mix together 500 μl Buffer FG2 (denaturation buffer) and 5 μl reconstituted QIAGEN Protease","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7t406","text":"Protease mixture should be prepared not more than 1 hour before use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1m2k0","text":"All centrifugation steps should be carried out at room temperature in a swing-out rotor using conical tubes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pipet 5 ml Buffer FG1 into a 10 ml centrifuge tube. Add 2 ml whole blood and mix by inverting the tube 5 times. undetermined, whole blood sample undetermined, Buffer FG1 Buffer FG1 (lysis buffer) Qiagen Catalog #51206
Centrifuge for 5 min at 2000 x g in a swing-out rotor. 00:05:00, Centrifugation

Invert the tube 3 times, place it in a heating block or water bath, and incubate at 65°C for 10 min. The sample changes color from red to olive green, indicating protein digestion. 00:10:00, Incubation at 65°C

Add 1 ml Buffer FG2/QIAGEN Protease, close the tube, and vortex immediately until the pellet is completely homogenized. Inspect the tube to check that homogenization is complete. When processing multiple samples, vortex each tube immediately after addition of Buffer FG2/QIAGEN Protease. Do not wait until buffer has been added to all samples before vortexing. Usually 3–4 pulses of high-speed vortexing for 5 s each are sufficient to homogenize the pellet. However, traces of pellet with a jelly-like consistency (often barely visible) may remain. If these traces are seen, add a further 300 μl Buffer FG2 and vortex again. undetermined, Buffer FG2/QIAGEN Protease Buffer FG2 (denaturation buffer) Qiagen Catalog #51206 Qiagen Protease Qiagen Catalog #51206

Discard the supernatant and leave the tube inverted on a clean sheet of absorbent paper for 2 min, taking care that the pellet remains in the tube. In rare cases the pellet may be loose, so pour slowly. Inverting the tube onto absorbent paper minimizes backflow of supernatant from the rim and sides of the tube onto the pellet. 00:02:00, leave tube inverted on a sheet of absorbent paper

Add 1 ml isopropanol (100%) and mix thoroughly by inversion until the DNA precipitate becomes visible as threads or a clump. Complete mixing with isopropanol is vital to precipitate the DNA and must be checked by inspection. For samples with very low white blood cell 2 counts, in which the DNA may not be visible, invert the tube at least 20 times. Isopropanol Contributed by users
Centrifuge for 3 min at 2000 x g. 00:03:00, Centrifugation If the resulting pellets are loose, centrifugation can be prolonged or a higher g-force can be used.

Discard the supernatant and briefly invert the tube onto a clean piece of absorbent paper, taking care that the pellet remains in the tube. In rare cases the pellet may be loose, so pour slowly. If the white blood cell count of the sample was sufficiently high, the DNA should be visible as a small white pellet.
Add 1 ml 70% ethanol and vortex for 5 s. 00:00:05, Vortex undetermined, 70% Ethanol Ethanol 70% Contributed by users
Centrifuge for 3 min at 2000 x g. 00:03:00, Centrifugation If the resulting pellets are loose, centrifugation can be prolonged or a higher g-force can be used.

Discard the supernatant and leave the tube inverted on a clean piece of absorbent paper for at least 5 min, taking care that the pellet remains in the tube. In rare cases the pellet may be loose, so pour slowly. Inverting the tube onto absorbent paper minimizes backflow of ethanol from the rim and sides of the tube onto the pellet. 00:05:00, leave the tube inverted on a piece of absorbent paper
Air-dry the DNA pellet until all the liquid has evaporated (at least 5 min). Avoid over-drying the DNA pellet, since over-dried DNA is very difficult to dissolve. 00:05:00, air-dry the pellet

Add 200 μl Buffer FG3, vortex for 5 s at low speed, and dissolve the DNA by incubating for 1 h at 65°C in a heating block or water bath. 01:00:00, Incubation at 65°C If the DNA is not completely dissolved, incubate the solution overnight at room temperature. If a reduced volume of Buffer FG3 is used, the incubation time may need to be prolonged. Buffer FG3 (hydration buffer) Qiagen Catalog #51206
creator: Eyleen Nabyla Alvarenga Niitsuma
